sections.paragraph:
Arab. ساحف-

(1) pr. i.q. סָחַב, סָחָה to sweep, to scrape, and more strongly, to sweep away, to scrape off; hence used of a shower which carries every thing before it, Pro 28:3. (Arabic سَاحِيفَةُ a violent, sweeping rain; سُاحَافُ a torrent.)

(2) to cast down to the ground (Syr. ܣܚܰܦ id.), whence-

Niphal, to be cast down, Jer 46:15.
